title: Technical Problem Solving, Coding, and Work-Sample Boundaries · Cumulative Campaign Review
summary: A delayed hiring review reconstructs technical problem solving, coding, and work-sample boundaries from target, claim, evidence, audience, action, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, consent, owner, and next-decision evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for observable technical interview practice system after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.
Externalize assumptions, alternatives, invariants, tradeoffs, checkpoints, uncertainty, and help requests without narrating every keystroke.
Build the smallest correct behavior, test examples and changed cases, inspect failures, then improve measured cost or design.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Problem Contract · changed case · Review Problem Contract
steps.instruction:
review case — A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The review variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.

Which action demonstrates Problem Contract without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Problem Contract · changed case · Review Problem Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation. For Problem Contract · changed case · Review Problem Contract, keep Problem Contract connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The review variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Problem Contract condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: EEOC Employment Discrimination and AI for Workers — Controls awareness that automated recruiting, screening, assessment, promotion, and pay tools can implicate protected rights and accommodation needs.

stimulus.title: Sample Boundary case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The review variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Evaluate take-home scope, ownership, confidentiality, accessibility, tool disclosure, deadline, compensation, and production-use risk before consent.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Every interview assignment is reasonable because participation is optional.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate take-home scope, ownership, confidentiality, accessibility, tool disclosure, deadline, compensation, and production-use risk before consent. Artifact under review: observable technical interview practice system.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Sample Boundary, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Sample Boundary.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Sample Boundary

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate take-home scope, ownership, confidentiality, accessibility, tool disclosure, deadline, compensation, and production-use risk before consent.
